Bio

An academic in the Faculty of Engineering at SIMAD University, serving as a Lecturer with a background in Telecommunication Engineering. His research interests include MTC/M2M/Internet of Things, D2D Communications, Blockchain Technology, Green Communications.  He has published several peer-reviewed articles and contributes to interdisciplinary research initiatives. He also teaches a range of engineering courses and participates in academic forums and collaborative research projects.

Bio

With more than ten years of expertise in process engineering, industrial gas operations, and higher education, Abdullahi Abdusamad Abdurahman is a committed chemical engineer. He graduated from Red Sea University with a bachelor’s degree in chemical engineering and Lincoln University College in Malaysia with a master’s degree in water resource management. Abdullahi has made significant contributions to Somalia’s LPG sector and taught chemistry, thermodynamics, and fluid mechanics as a lecturer at various universities. He has both academic and practical experience in environmental impact analysis, sustainable engineering techniques.

Education Information
  • Master of Water Resource Management – Lincoln University College, Malaysia, October, 2023
  • Bachelor of Chemical Engineering – Red Sea University, Sudan, November, 2008
Research Area

  • Water supply systems and chemistry

  • Heat and mass transfer in multiphase systems

  • Advanced water and wastewater treatment

  • Impact of climate change on hydrologic cycles

  • Nature-based solutions for water purification (e.g., wetlands bio filters)

  • Flood forecasting and mitigation strategies.

Courses

Projects & Grants
  • February 2009–2013: Engineer for GAZWAL (LP Gas Company): It primarily entails refilling and repairing gas cylinders as well as offloading LPG gas from trucks to main store tanks. Ensuring quality control of the LPG gas and maintaining safety precautions for the company, Juba-South Sudan
  • Since 2014, I have been working as a consultant and production engineer for SOM GAS (LP Gas Distribution Co.). My work mainly involves the refilling and repairing of gas cylinders and the offloading of LPG gas from the storage tanks to the production unit. Ensuring quality control of the LPG gas and maintaining safety precautions for the company.
  • Since 2012, I have been a lecturer at Mogadishu University, Faculty of Engineering, teaching general chemistry, fluid mechanics, thermodynamics.
  • Since 2014, I have been a lecturer at SIMAD University, Faculty of Engineering, teaching engineering chemistry and fluid mechanics.
  • Since 2018, I have been a lecturer in the Faculty of Engineering at Jamhuuriya University, teaching fluid mechanics.
Industrial and workshops
  • In 2006, I spent four weeks at the Port Sudan Oil Refinery/Port Sudan, Sudan. Analyze specifications, schedules, logs of tests, results, and laboratory recommendations to determine how to set equipment controls to produce the required qualities and quantities of products.
  • Six-week period completed Municipal Water and Waste Water Treatment in Port Sudan, Sudan, 2007. Water and wastewater treatment plant operators increasingly rely on computers to help monitor equipment, store sampling results to make process control decisions, schedule operations, and record production reports.
  • Three-week period completed in Sudan’s New Halfa Factory Sugar, 2008. Familiarize yourself with the sugar manufacturing process from start to finish.
  • In 2008, I completed a four-week internship at the Atbara Cement Factory in Atbara, Sudan. Introduction to the cement manufacturing process, including all steps such as quality control, component mixing, and packaging to create the finished product.
  • In August 2016, I completed a three-day training course on chemical safety and security in Antananarivo, Madagascar.
  • In February 2017, I completed a three-day workshop training course on Chemical Risk Management for Industry in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.

  2. Impact of seasonal flooding on building damages in Mogadishu, Somalia
  3. Sustainable irrigation practices and their impact on water resources in semi-arid regions: case study of Galgadud region.

Bio

Abdirahman Ali Muse is a committed academic and transportation engineer with an MSc in
Highway and Transportation Engineering from Universiti Putra Malaysia. He serves as a lecturer
at several Somali universities, contributing to academic development through both teaching and
research. His research interests include transportation planning, traffic flow modelling, road
safety, and meta-analysis techniques, and he has published in peer-reviewed international
journals. With a background in civil engineering and IT, he combines technical expertise with a
dedication to student mentorship and academic innovation.

A senior academic in the field of Civil Engineering with a M.Sc. in Water Engineering from University Putra Malaysia. Their research focuses on water infrastructure development, environmental risk assessment, and sustainable Water quality. With over fifteen years of teaching and research experience, they have delivered undergraduate courses in water engineering, hydrology, hydraulics and environmental geoscience. The academic is actively involved in supervising graduate research projects and contributing to interdisciplinary studies that address real-world challenges in water resource management. Their research has been presented at regional and international academic conferences and published in peer-reviewed journals, with a focus on water resource, geoscience, safety engineering, and the integration of local knowledge into environmental planning.

Bio

Dr. M. A. Fiidow obtained his doctorate from Universiti Putra Malaysia, a prestigious university known for its rigorous academic programmes. His research focused on abstract algebra, specifically delving into areas like Pure and applied Mathematics. Also, he is Head of the Department of Mathematics at the Somali National University, Dr. Fiidow plays a crucial role in shaping the next generation of mathematicians in Somalia. His dedication to education extends beyond lectures, as he actively seeks to make mathematics an engaging and inspiring subject for his students.
Dr. M. A. Fiidow is a strong advocate for research in Somalia. He collaborates with other researchers, both within Somalia and internationally, to foster a vibrant research environment and contribute to the country’s intellectual growth.
Dr. M. A. Fiidow’s work transcends borders. He actively seeks to establish collaborations and exchange programs with mathematicians from other countries, opening doors for Somali students and researchers to connect with the global scientific community.
Dr. M. A. Fiidow is a remarkable individual who embodies the multifaceted nature of a modern scholar. His dedication to both academic excellence and social impact makes him a true asset to Somalia and the wider mathematical community.

About

Full Info

SIMAD was established as an institute on November 6, 1999, and it began its operations enthusiastically. This trajectory reached a significant milestone on January 20, 2011, when the Board of Trustees of the former Somali Institute of Management and Administration Development (SIMAD) approved its transformation into a full-fledged university.

Before this transformative decision, our faculty comprised the Accounting and Business Administration programs, which later evolved into the Faculty of Business and Accountancy. However, in 2015, the top management of SIMAD University decided to rebrand the faculty, resulting in the birth of the Faculty of Management Sciences. This change aimed to reflect the broader range of disciplines and expertise offered within the faculty.

To expand our academic offerings further, we introduced two additional programs. In 2013, the Bachelor of Banking and Finance program was launched, providing a comprehensive understanding of the financial industry. Subsequently, in 2014, the Bachelor of Procurement and Logistics program was introduced, equipping students with the necessary skills to excel in procurement and supply chain management.

As our faculty continued to thrive, we remained dedicated to staying at the forefront of education. In 2023, we proudly expanded our program portfolio once again. Two exciting additions were made: the Bachelor of Digital Marketing program, which focuses on the dynamic world of online marketing, and the Bachelor of Entrepreneurship and Innovation program, which is designed to nurture the next generation of innovative entrepreneurs.

Today, the Faculty of Management Sciences is one of the largest and most prominent faculties within SIMAD University. It offers a comprehensive range of five programs: Business Administration, Banking and Finance, Procurement and Logistics, Entrepreneurship & Innovation, and Digital Marketing. This diverse array of programs caters to students’ varied interests and aspirations, equipping them with the knowledge and skills required to thrive in the ever-changing management field.

Scholarships and Financial Aid

We believe that studying for a degree at SIMAD University is a sound investment for your future. However, we understand that the cost of attending university can seem a daunting prospect. We offer scholarships and financial aid to prospective and current students in the form of academic merit scholarship, student loan (Qardhul Hasan), and Quran Scholarship. Here is the explanation of all the types of scholarship or financial aid available.

Academic Merit Scholarship 

This scholarship is awarded to faculty outstanding students based on their academic performance. All faculty outstanding students will have the opportunity to study the next semester free at the University. The scope of this scholarship is only for the next semester. To be eligible for this type of scholarship, students must be first admitted at the university and be inspired to work hard at studies to achieve excellence in academic and co-curricular and extra-curricular activities.

Quran Scholarship

The University holds Quran contents every year and all university students, both females and males, can participate in this competition. Students will be asked to read some verses of the holy Quran and the University awards full scholarships to the top performing students. Each year, one female and one male student are chosen based on their performance in Quran competition and are awarded full scholarship until the completion of their studies. The university strongly encourages students to read and learn the Quran by heart. To be eligible for this scholarship, prospective students should first register at the university and wait for the cultural week, where so many contests including Quran is held.

Qardhul Hasan

Qardhul Hasan is a kind of scholarship or interest- free student loan program available at SIMAD University. The university strongly believes that every student has the right to attain higher education regardless of their financial background. To ensure this, the university, in collaboration with Direct Aid and Premier Bank, introduced this type of scholarship aimed to help Somali students to pursue their studies without worrying about financial difficulties. Students will only pay 30% of the tuition fee every semester and the remaining 70% will be repaid by the student within three years after graduation. To be considered for this scholarship, students should first enroll at the university and then submit a Qardhul Hasan request to the Students Affairs Office.

Studying at SIMAD

Student-centered Learning

Students are key in implementing teaching and learning effectiveness at SIMAD. The curriculum strives to cater to different learning styles of students. Student-centered learning encompasses all teaching and learning methods that aim to move the focus of the learning away from the instructor to the student to engage them, motivate them, and give them voice and ownership in the learning process. The guiding methodology for teaching and learning stems from a student-centered approach that engages students in the learning process in their pursuit to become readers, writers, thinkers and above all doers.

Students are encouraged to immerse themselves in this rich learning environment by questioning and re-questioning as they explore their learning journeys and discover their career paths. Our learning programs have been designed to equip you with essential tools for success: critical thinking, effective communication skills, collaboration and teamwork skills and creativity as well as career and life skills. 

Classrooms 

We create adequate, relaxed learning environment that is conducive to learning with ample learning and technology support. Our classes have been designed to cater to different types of teaching styles and classroom activities.

Collaborative Learning Style

At SIMAD, classroom lectures, in the traditional sense, have virtually ceased to exist. Instead, learning sessions are conducted in a dynamic and collaborative manner where students and professors exchange ideas, debate concepts and learn together. Professors passionately introduce the topic under study and map out learning strategies to stimulate students’ intellectual curiosity and interest in the topic. 

Independent Study

Independent study or self-directed study is one of the key features that characterize SU learning. The university systematically incorporates autonomous learning in its programs to promote learning that continues beyond its classrooms. For graduates with extensive breadth of knowledge base and deeper understanding of subjects they study, SIMAD University gives its students many opportunities to develop a love for reading and studying independently.

Student Support

In addition to attending lectures, our fresh students will have opportunities to attend tutorials or tutoring programs. The tutorial is led by a senior or a top outstanding student. Fresh students will have the chances to learn more from other outstanding students, talk about points of interests from the lectures and the readings, get help from tutors in doing exercises or assignments. 

Co-curricular Programs

Co-curricular activity (CCA) is one of the university’s key functions aimed at developing students both academically and conceptually. We offer students right tools, skills, and knowledge to help them make the best possible career decisions. This generally enhances the students on many aspects ranging from expanding the students’ horizon, their skills on computers, their skills to participate talks, debates, and seminars. Through CCA, students will discover their interests and talents while developing values and competencies that will prepare them for a rapidly changing world. CCA also will promote friendships among students from diverse backgrounds as they learn, play, and grow together. Participation in CCA will foster social integration and deepens students’ sense of belonging, commitment, and sense of responsibility to school, community, and nation.

Student Exchange and Mobility Programs

At SIMAD, our students can participate in student exchange programs. Student exchange programs provide students with an opportunity to study one or two semesters local or overseas at our partner universities. SU exchange programs help give our students international exposure and internal encounters that will be helpful in their careers, a new and more sophisticated perspective on community issues, and to enhance their cross-cultural experience and knowledge, for example seeing how their profession is practiced in another country. After they return home from even one semester of exchange, students may be different than before and stick out from their peers due to their special experiences and skills. These programs also give students a chance to travel abroad while completing their degree.

Academic Support

For students to succeed in their courses, SIMAD University lecturers ensure that students know what is expected of them as well as the resources available. Lecturers provide students with constructive feedback that guides their efforts towards achievement. Course objectives and the specific learning outcomes inform decisions when designing/redesigning exams and other assignments.

Research Projects/Lab Work 

As you move closer to graduation, you will be required to produce an extended, independent research project which culminates in an honors thesis. This important work will be undertaken with the guidance of a designated professor who is an expert in your chosen field of study. Students are fully supported by our approachable and friendly library and lab personnel while conducting their research in our libraries and other research facilities on campus.

Student-led Startups 

Maybe you are a potential entrepreneur, and always thinking of having own business? IITE Institute is there for you. To ensure that our students and graduates receive practical entrepreneurship skills and contribute to the wider development of Somalia, SIMAD launched IITE Institute to serve as a hub for nurturing innovation, technology, and entrepreneurship education in Somalia. The institute provides project-based learning, workshops, and problem-solving case-study competitions to boost local innovation and creativity. It gives mentorship to ﬁnal-year students with entrepreneurship and technology education that prepares them for wage employment, freelancing, or start-up establishment, and trains Somali executives, middle managers, and frontline staff with certiﬁed training packages. The institute focuses on capacity building on a personal level, encourages and launches student-led startups, develops an innovation-driven entrepreneurship ecosystem in Somalia, and promotes student-lead business initiatives that address local challenges.

SIMAD Research Repository

SIMAD University Research Repository is a dedicated digital platform that serves as a comprehensive collection of academic research produced by the university’s faculties, students, and researchers. This repository facilitates the storage, preservation, and sharing of scholarly research papers. By providing open access to these materials, it enhances the visibility and impact of SIMAD University’s research outputs, fostering a culture of knowledge sharing and collaboration within the academic community. The platform supports the university’s commitment to advancing education, research, and innovation across different fields of study. The Faculty of Management Science section of the repository showcases research from faculty and students within management and business disciplines, featuring studies on management practices, entrepreneurship, organizational behavior, economics, and other related areas, highlighting the faculty’s dedication to producing high-quality research that contributes to both academic and practical advancements in the field of management.

Internet Access

High-speed Internet access is available across campus. Students can access global electronic journals, databases, and digital resources via a reliable fiber-optic lines and VSAT technology.

Computer Labs

Our computer labs are available across our campuses to support and enrich the learning experience of our students. The extensive technology infrastructure and the wide range of technology support systems give students the opportunity to gain hands-on practice working with technology. Students, faculty, and staff are welcomed to drop in at any time during university’s working hours.

SIMAD iLab

To create a culture of student startups, SIMAD University established IITE Institute, a fully-fledged innovation hub that incubates campus entrepreneurs. The institute has achieved great milestones within two years by producing 18 startups, accelerating 47 MSMEs, and training young 387 entrepreneurs.

Now, to scale up the reach and impact of the institute, SIMAD University has introduced SIMAD iLab to host IITE Institute. SIMAD iLab is a well-equipped coworking space that is dedicated to the Mogadishu innovation community. The iLab has a FabLab, the first digital fabrication space in Somalia; a coworking space; a conference room for startup competitions; napping room for enhancing productivity; and world-class seminar rooms.

SIMAD Mosque

As part to its commitment to create an environment that supports holistic wellbeing, SU mosque plays an integral part in enhancing wellness and promotes peace from within across our campuses. It is that special place where our students go for spiritual renewal and intellectual nourishment. Strategically located near the student center, the campus mosque has separate prayer halls for both brothers and sisters. It also serves as a great place where you meet new people or catch up with old friends.

Catering Services

SU has a diverse range of catering outlets across our campuses. Our cafes and restaurants, which are conveniently located right on campus offer food choices whether you want to grab something on the move or fancy to treat yourself for a more proper dining experience, rest assured that you will find something that meets your individual needs. Whether you crave a samosa, or you want to order your favorite spaghetti dish or you just want a cup of tea with cinnamon or cardamom, you will find something that whets your appetite. The university’s catering services ensures that students, faculty, and staff continue to receive best value for their money at pocket friendly prices.

Events & Activities

Cultural Week

SIMAD University Culture Week is yet another exciting and truly education annual event which is held on campus. It is a weeklong full of activities that highlight the best of Somali poetry and literature. This is complemented by a series of panel discussions, traditional performances, and sport tournaments.

Sports

Knowledge transfer is one of the main objectives of SIMAD University, but we are also aware of the impact social activities outside the class room can have on our students. For this purpose, we organize sports events of various kinds to our students. 

Student Government

As part of its governance system, SIMAD University allows students to run their own affairs in a democratic manner. XAJSi is there to enhance every aspect of your time as a student – supporting your social life, your studies, your sporting activities, your hobbies and interests, and the causes you care about. You could also be elected as a Faculty Rep, or even as a Student President, to help drive improvements across the University. Taking part in student association and clubs for your course and Student Government (XAJSi) forums and discussions makes a real difference and helps build your CV.

Communication Service Day

In line with our commitment to providing thoughtful and impactful community services, SU Community Service Day aims to provide students with opportunities to reach out and make a difference in the local communities. Although SIMAD University is engaged tirelessly in community outreach daily, the Community Service Day is a special day in which we exhibit our ongoing care and compassion to people in our neighborhoods.

Health & Safety

Student Wellbeing

SIMAD places student wellbeing at the center of its core services. We care about our students’ overall wellbeing by recognizing them not just as students but also as human beings with unique needs, challenges, goals, and emotions. Student wellness is holistic and we therefore consider all aspects of student life: physical, mental, and spiritual. They are involved in various sport activities and they can go to the mosques around campus for meditation, prayers and for spiritual reawakening. Our students are couched to ease their mental stress and develop their ability to adapt and effectively deal with academic and life challenges. 

Through various activities and services, SU students lead a happy life and have the support that cultivates the confidence they need to realize their full potential. Our students work consistently on their life goals and have a sense of purpose in life. Throughout their academic career at SU, our students develop success skills of being resilient, independent, well-rounded, and balanced individuals. 

Student Health Services

As part of students’ wellbeing, the university provides health benefits plan to its undergraduate students. Students enrolled at SIMAD University will have an opportunity to receive medical discounts at Dr. Sumait Hospital. We promote a healthy environment for learning by offering quality health services to current students.

Dr.Sumait Hospital

SIMAD University has been at the forefront of education in Somalia for twenty years, on various fronts; one of them is providing excellent medical education and healthcare services to the Somali people. The establishment of SIMAD University Dr. Sumait Hospital is meant to help fulﬁll both educational and healthcare necessities.

A medical school requires a well-equipped teaching hospital to train students during their clerkship and intern-ship years. Somali medical students are under profound travel restrictions, and so world-class medical education and training need to be conducted at home and will require teaching hospitals to produce highly qualiﬁed doctors to better serve the Somali community. Additionally, a well-equipped tertiary hospital can provide a comprehensive medical care for Somali patients, who are under heavy travel restrictions as well, and reduce need to travel abroad for medical treatment.

Message from the Dean:

The Faculty of Engineering is dedicated to quality, innovation, and sustainability.  Our methodology is caring and collaborative, emphasizing the integration of research and teaching across all pertinent domains.  We prioritize skills-oriented and fundamentals-based education, guaranteeing that our curriculum is inclusive and adaptable to both local and global concerns.

 We recognize that for future progress, engineering and built environment specialists must comprehend the influence of technology on the sector.  Our students are urged to understand the many demands of the world in which we reside.  This viewpoint is essential for envisioning, developing, and maintaining a future that benefits future generations.

 Interdisciplinary collaboration is central to our pedagogy and research endeavors.  We are committed to delivering tailored educational experiences and AI-enhanced solutions, bolstered by innovative environments and resources.  Our emphasis on digital equity and uninterrupted connectivity, along with optimized administrative procedures, guarantees our leadership in technological and educational progress.

 We shall consistently endeavor to foster relationships and sustain a collaborative atmosphere, equipping our students and staff to lead and create presently and in the future.  The faculty’s work encompasses health, energy, sustainable housing, circular economy, artificial intelligence, cyber security and communication, water shortage, and additional areas.

Abdikarim Abi Hassan
Dean Faculty of Engineering

About the Faculty

Faculty of Engineering

SIMAD University’s Faculty of Engineering is committed to advancing engineering education and training in Somalia. With an emphasis on academic excellence, research, and practical experience, the faculty equips students with the technical skills, innovation, and ethical grounding needed to excel in engineering fields.

Our programs blend modern teaching techniques with hands-on learning, preparing graduates to be leaders who contribute meaningfully to local and global engineering challenges.

Mission & Vision

At the Faculty of Engineering, we are driven by a strong sense of purpose: to transform engineering education through innovation, research, and societal impact. Our commitment is to prepare future engineers who are not only academically proficient but also ethically grounded and socially responsible. By combining rigorous academic training, hands-on experience, and active industry engagement, we aim to produce engineers who can address real-world challenges and contribute to building sustainable, innovative, and equitable solutions for the future.

FAQ

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

Below, you will find the most asked questions related to FENG.

What programs are offered at the Faculty of Engineering?

The Faculty of Engineering at SIMAD University offers a diverse range of undergraduate programs including Civil Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Telecommunications Engineering, Architecture, and City & Regional Planning. These programs provide students with the necessary skills to excel in various fields of engineering and contribute to the development of Somalia and beyond.

How long do the degree programs take to complete?

Degree programs at the Faculty of Engineering typically take 5 years (10 semesters) to complete. Each program is designed to ensure that students gain a solid foundation in engineering principles, as well as practical experience in their chosen field.

What are the admission requirements for the Faculty of Engineering?

To be admitted to the Faculty of Engineering, applicants must meet SIMAD University’s general entry requirements. This includes having completed secondary school with strong results in subjects such as Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry. Additional specific program requirements may apply depending on the chosen field of study.

Are there any practical training or industry attachments during study?

Yes, the Faculty of Engineering places a strong emphasis on hands-on learning. Students are provided with opportunities for internships, fieldwork, and industry placements to apply their knowledge and gain practical experience in real-world engineering projects.

Is there any financial aid or scholarship opportunity?

SIMAD University offers various scholarship opportunities to eligible students based on academic performance and financial need. These scholarships aim to make higher education more accessible and support students in their educational journey.

How does the Faculty of Engineering support student research and innovation?

The Faculty of Engineering actively promotes student research and innovation through partnerships, research projects, and access to modern labs and facilities. Students are encouraged to engage in research that addresses real-world engineering challenges, contributing to the development of sustainable and innovative solutions.
